evals.prompt: I have 16 repository assessment reports in the assessments/ directory under /Users/lionelrivas/develop-better/development/cross-repository-synthesis/. These are all current-format reports that include a Synthesis Input Summary section. Use the cross-repository engineering synthesis skill to normalize each report, identify recurring engineering patterns across the portfolio, produce standards candidates with code-example guidance, draft learning recommendations, identify repository-specific remediation candidates, and write an executive summary. Save all output files to /Users/lionelrivas/develop-better/development/cross-repository-synthesis-workspace/iteration-1/full-synthesis-{config}/outputs/ where {config} is replaced by your configuration label.
evals.expected_output: Six output files: cross-repository-engineering-findings-synthesis.md, normalized-assessment-summaries.md, engineering-standards-candidates.md, learning-recommendations.md, repository-remediation-candidates.md, synthesis-executive-summary.md — all following the correct templates, with standards candidates containing code-example plans for high-priority items, correct use of risk-pattern taxonomy tags, and improvement-oriented tone throughout.

assertions.description: All 6 required output files are created and non-empty
Standards candidates include labeled Problem Example, Preferred Example, and Testing Example sections
Each learning recommendation specifies a recommended learning format (e.g. workshop, kata, walkthrough)
Each learning recommendation includes a before example and an after example
The engineering standards candidates file includes a summary table at the top
The executive summary is concise (under 400 words) and leadership-appropriate
